best_config_overall.note: lambda_align=2.0 stabilizes proxy alignment, preventing backdoor re-emergence during simultaneous safe training

alternating_best_config.note: Meets ASR <= 0.15 but higher benign_fr than best config

new_experiments_this_session.label: alternating_soft_s25_l008
new_experiments_this_session.diagnosis: diluted safe signal hurts ASR badly

new_experiments_this_session.label: alternating_then_simul_s25_l008
new_experiments_this_session.diagnosis: simultaneous after alternating undoes ASR gains

new_experiments_this_session.label: simul_l009_s25
new_experiments_this_session.diagnosis: safe > 0.08 at s25 also degrades ASR (not just at s30)

new_experiments_this_session.label: simul_l008_align2_s25
new_experiments_this_session.diagnosis: BEST — stronger alignment stabilizes against backdoor; meets early stop

new_experiments_this_session.label: simul_l008_align1.5_s25
new_experiments_this_session.diagnosis: nonlinear — 1.5 not enough, 2.0 hits phase transition

key_insights: lambda_align=2.0 hits a phase transition where proxy alignment stabilizes representations against backdoor re-emergence
Any dilution of safe isolation (soft, 2c1s, soft+2c1s, then_simul) causes ASR to spike to 0.35-0.44
lambda_safe > 0.08 degrades ASR regardless of step count — the safe objective interferes with alignment
Alternating schedule achieves lowest ASR (0.092) but at 48% benign_fr — the binary response mode is fundamental
Best balanced config: simultaneous + l_align=2.0 + l_safe=0.08 + s25 → ASR=0.142 (meets early stop), benign_fr=0.39
